root_cause_summary: Soft lockup on CPU#2 combined with a concurrent SError Interrupt (ESR_EL1=0xBE000000, EC=0x2F) indicates a hardware-level asynchronous error causing both the CPU stall and the kernel panic.
evidence: BUG: soft lockup - CPU#2 stuck for 22s! [kworker/2:1:456]
Kernel panic - not syncing: Asynchronous SError Interrupt

sop_steps.instruction: Run check_cache_coherency_panic on the full dmesg to confirm SError origin. Verify dcache flush sequence during CPU suspend/resume.
sop_steps.expected_value: Identify whether cache coherency failure preceded the soft lockup.

sop_steps.instruction: Scope PMIC rails (VDDCPU, VDDMX) during the boot/stress period. ESR EC=0x2F SError can be triggered by DRAM ECC errors under insufficient power.
sop_steps.expected_value: All rails within ±5% of nominal voltage during load.
